title: cyclopogon olivaceus
intro: Cyclopogon olivaceus, also known as the 'Olive Spiked Anthera', is a type of flowering plant native to Central and South America, characterized by its slender stems and small white or yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cyclopogon olivaceus include relief from fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
